steps.title: Identify the rhetorical goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize whose point of view the novel is told from. The correct choice must name the novel and explicitly identify Kaikeyi as the perspective character.

steps.title: Scan the choices for that emphasis
steps.content: Only one choice both names the novel Kaikeyi and states "from the character Kaikeyi's point of view" — making POV the focus.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'From the point of view of The Ramayana, the character Kaikeyi is often a villain': this is about the epic poem, not the novel, and misuses "point of view" to mean opinion. Misses the goal. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Vaishnavi Patel often portrays the character as heroic': emphasizes the author's characterization, not whose POV the novel uses. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Kaikeyi is a retelling of The Ramayana from the character Kaikeyi's point of view': directly names the novel and the POV character. Hits the goal. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The Ramayana is an epic poem that features the character Kaikeyi': describes the source poem; says nothing about the novel's point of view. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
